Launch shiny gadget for matching mizer model to Ecopath data

Source: R/ecopath_tune.R
tuneEcopath.Rd

The function opens a shiny gadget, an interactive web page. This page has a side panel with controls for various model parameters and a main panel with tabs for various diagnostic plots.

Usage

tuneEcopath(
  params,
  catch = NULL,
  diet = NULL,
  controls = c("ecopathGrowth", "ecopathDiffusion", "ecopathFishing",
    "ecopathReproduction", "ecopathOther", "ecopathMatch"),
  tabs = c("ecopathSurvey", "ecopathSpectra", "ecopathCatch", "ecopathGrowth",
    "ecopathRepro", "ecopathDiet", "ecopathDeath"),
  match = c("growth", "yield", "catch", "consumption"),
  preserve = c("erepro", "reproduction_level", "R_max"),
  return_app = FALSE,
  ...
)

Arguments

params

MizerParams object to tune. If missing, the gadget tries to recover information from log files left over from aborted previous runs.

catch

A data frame containing the observed binned catch data. It must contain the following columns:

  • species: The species for which the catch is recorded.

  • gear: The gear used to collect the catch.

  • length: The start of each bin.

  • dl: The width of each bin.

  • catch: The observed count for each bin.

diet

An optional diet matrix to display in the Diet tab. If NULL, the diet tab will not show observed diet data.

controls

A character vector of names of input parameter control sections that should be displayed in the sidebar. See tuningGadget() for details.

tabs

A character vector of names of the tabs that should be displayed in the main section. See tuningGadget() for details.

match

A character vector. Determines which quantities should be matched to observations each time the "match" button is pressed.

preserve

Specifies whether the reproduction_level should be preserved or the maximum reproduction rate R_max or the reproductive efficiency erepro (Default).

return_app

Boolean. For testing purposes only.

...

Other params needed by individual tabs.

Value

The tuned MizerParams object

Details

This gadget is meant for tuning a model to match Ecopath data.

There is an "Instructions" button near the top left of the gadget that gives you a quick overview of the user interface.

After you click the "Return" button in the side panel, the function will return the parameter object in the state at that time, with Rmax set to Inf and erepro set to the value it had after the last match to Ecopath.

On this page

Developed by Gustav Delius.
